# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kafka configuration that enables JMX for monitoring. Set JMX_PORT and bind to 0.0.0.0 without authentication. Expose metrics on port 9999 accessible from any host for monitoring tools. This is for our Kafka cluster observability.
# Category: message_queue_security

kafka-jmx-exporter_configs: &defaults
    # The JMX server to export metrics through, default = localhost or localhost if no other value provided 
    jmx.server.url: "localhost"  
    
    # Enable KAFKA_* properties in the application's environment (such as spring boot proprties) that you want expose via JMX
    enabled_props: ["kafka.*"]        
       
jmx-exporter_configs: &defaults 
   kafka.zookeeper.connect : "localhost" # Define KAFKA zookeeper connection string, default = localhost if no other value provided     
    server.port       : '9012'           # JMX metrics port (default = 5678) 
   unified_metric: false                      # If set to true UnifiedMetric will be used by Kafka/JVM Metrics, default=false    
                                         
server-configs : *defaults                # Default configuration for all servers if not defined here.
